channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Philippines Reports Cyanide Found on Chinese Boats at Ayungin Shoal
item.description: The Philippines reported the discovery of cyanide on Chinese vessels operating near the disputed Ayungin Shoal (Second Thomas Shoal) in the South China Sea. Philippine security officials confirmed tha...

item.title: US launches limited naval blockade on Iranian ports as ceasefire talks collapse
item.description: The article reports that US President Donald Trump has ordered a naval blockade targeting vessels entering and leaving Iranian ports after marathon ceasefire talks between Washington and Tehran in Isl...

item.title: Finland Prepares Deep Geological Repository for Spent Nuclear Fuel with Long-Term Safety Considerations
item.description: Finland is set to open the world's first permanent facility for disposing of spent nuclear fuel, called Onkalo, located on the island of Olkiluoto. Construction began in 2004, and the project, costing...

item.title: Manas Mangaraj appointed BJD’s leader in Rajya Sabha amid internal reorganisation
item.description: The Biju Janata Dal (BJD) has announced significant leadership changes in its Rajya Sabha contingent following the resignation of senior member Sasmit Patra. On Monday, party president Naveen Patnaik ...

item.title: US-Iran Talks in Islamabad: Pakistan's Role in Conflict Resolution
item.description: Recent discussions between the United States and Iran, facilitated by Pakistan in Islamabad, have concluded without a major breakthrough, but also without a complete breakdown, according to Pakistani ...

item.title: Miss World Tanzania 2026: 20 Contestants Vie for Crown and Global Representation
item.description: Mambo! Get ready, Tanzania! Twenty beautiful ladies are all set to battle it out for the Miss World Tanzania 2026 crown. The grand finale, which promises to be a real spectacle, will be held on Sunday...

item.title: Trump’s defamation lawsuit against The Wall Street Journal dismissed by Florida judge
item.description: A US federal judge in Florida has dismissed Donald Trump’s $15 billion defamation lawsuit against Rupert Murdoch and The Wall Street Journal. The case centred on a July report claiming that Trump had ...

item.title: Bishop Kimengich urges government to address political violence
item.description: Bishop Dominic Kimengich of the Catholic Diocese of Eldoret has called on the Kenyan government to intervene and restore order following recent attacks on politicians and critics by goons. Speaking in...

item.title: Rain and Thunderstorms Expected Across Upper Pakistan from April 16-19
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued a forecast for rain and thunderstorms across the upper regions of Pakistan, specifically from April 16th to April 19th. A westerly wave is antic...

item.title: Mother jailed after driving drunk at high speed with children in car and crashing in Cheadle
item.description: A 50-year-old mother from Cheadle, Faye Dawson, has been jailed after pleading guilty to dangerous driving following a serious crash involving several children. According to the report, she was filmed...

item.title: Families of Baby Pendo and other brutality victims condemn decision to free eight police bosses in Kisumu case
item.description: The Baby Pendo murder case has once again stirred public anger after the release of eight senior police commanders who were facing charges over the death of the six-month-old baby during protests in K...

item.title: Cuban President Affirms Commitment to Defend Nation Amid U.S. Tensions
item.description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel stated in an interview on NBC News' Meet the Press that Cuba would defend itself in the event of a U.S. invasion. He emphasized that the United States has no legitima...

item.title: Assessing the Health Consequences of Rising Sea Levels and Responsibility for Costs
item.description: Recent research highlights the escalating health impacts caused by rising sea levels, which are already affecting physical and mental health, livelihoods, and cultural practices around the world. An i...

item.title: Data Breach at Anodot Exposes Customer Companies to Extortion Attempts
item.description: A recent cyberattack on business monitoring software provider Anodot has resulted in the theft of sensitive data from at least a dozen of its corporate clients, leaving these companies vulnerable to e...

item.title: Geely launches AI-driven hybrid system aiming to compete with Japanese models
item.description: Geely has officially launched its i-HEV Intelligent Hybrid system, signalling a bold move to compete with Japanese hybrid vehicles in the conventional hybrid market. The new system integrates an AI-dr...

item.title: Kiko Barzaga turns himself in over multiple cyber libel charges
item.description: Kiko Barzaga, a public figure in the Philippines, has voluntarily surrendered to authorities in connection with eight counts of cyber libel. The charges stem from online posts that allegedly contained...

item.title: Red Line BRT construction in Karachi faces renewed delays due to financial and coordination issues
item.description: The construction of Karachi’s Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) Red Line project has once again slowed down significantly, causing frustration among citizens and raising questions about the efficiency of projec...

item.title: U.S. Threatens Action Against Iranian Ships Amid Strait of Hormuz Tensions
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East are escalating as the United States prepares to impose a blockade on Iranian ports, including those near the strategically important Strait of Hormuz. Former U.S. President...

item.title: Ruby Rose Publicly Accuses Katy Perry of Sexual Assault, Details Past Efforts to Protect Herself
item.description: Actor and model Ruby Rose has made serious allegations of sexual assault against singer Katy Perry, sharing her experience publicly for the first time after nearly two decades of silence. Rose reveale...

item.title: Winnipeg woman sued over alleged misappropriation of $6M from Indigenous Guardians non-profit
item.description: A Winnipeg resident, Melanie Desjarlais, is facing a lawsuit filed by the First Nations National Guardians Network (NGN), a non-profit that previously managed federal funds for Indigenous conservation...
